reviewer: solo new-family build 2026-07-21 — eDifFIQA(T) runnability + correctness proven FIRST in headless Chrome (the real ONNX score fell monotonically with image degradation), then the deployed page driven end-to-end (six licensed portraits scored + ranked; blur-8 dropped a face 0.386 -> 0.096). Sample faces are the ledgered licensed media/assets/face-* portraits, cropped client-side.

rubric.evidence: RUNNABILITY + CORRECTNESS verified in headless Chrome (onnxruntime-web 1.21.0, WASM EP, no GPU) BEFORE building. eDifFIQA(T) fp32 (7.3 MB) builds a session (input [1,3,112,112], output [1,1]) and runs ~14 ms/face. On a licensed portrait the score fell MONOTONICALLY with degradation: sharp 0.431, blur-3 0.138, blur-8 0.106. Deployed page (scripts/validate-face-iqa.mjs 6/6): six portraits scored + ranked descending, blur-8 dropped a face 0.386 -> 0.096. The score tracks capture quality — not a canned number.
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in a module Worker. Tiny model (~7 MB) + fast (~14 ms), so scoring a whole gallery + a live degrade slider is comfortable.

rubric.evidence: Aligned face crop [1,3,112,112] float32 in 0-1 -> [1,1] scalar quality (higher = more usable capture). A DISTINCT capability: it JUDGES a face capture (sharp/lit/frontal enough?) rather than detecting, landmarking, or identifying it (all built separately). Face-quality gating is a standard first stage in biometric pipelines.
rubric.notes: The demo scales the raw score to a bar (observed range ~0.1-0.45) and always shows the raw number so the value isn't misrepresented as a calibrated 0-1 probability.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (opencv/face_image_quality_assessment_ediffiqa, linked), task (face-image-quality), runtime (onnxruntime-web 1.21 — noting transformers.js has no face-IQA class/task), the real I/O shapes, quantisation (fp32), backend (WASM), download (~7 MB) and license (Apache-2.0, OpenCV Zoo). No invented API.
rubric.notes: onnxruntime-web is pinned in THIS worker only (isolated per-worker escape hatch, like raft/reid); shared lib/webai.js untouched.

rubric.evidence: Loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model {modelId:'opencv/face_image_quality_assessment_ediffiqa', runtime:'onnxruntime-web', dtype:'fp32', sizeMB:7}). Weights fetched THROUGH Cache Storage (face-image-quality-onnx-cache) so lib/model-cache.js scans '/opencv/face_image_quality_assessment_ediffiqa/' -> a returning visit auto-initialises with NO Download click; first visit shows an explicit Download (~7 MB). Verified: fresh profile showed Download, clicking it flipped to ready.
rubric.notes: Small model so re-download is cheap, but the never-silently-download guarantee + auto-init on return still hold via the shared loader; a per-model clear-cache control is offered once ready.

rubric.evidence: checking -> Download (absent) -> ready (scores + ranks the gallery, primes the degrade preview) -> live re-score on slider input; error path via the shared loader. Verified download -> ready -> gallery scored -> degrade sequence end-to-end.
rubric.notes: PENDING: the basics/practical/wild/multi-model ladder is not yet built (index only). Recorded in followUpGoals.

rubric.evidence: A face <select> plus native range sliders for Blur (0-8 px) and Darken (0-70%) with live value labels; the preview + score update on every input. The ranked gallery is read-only score cards.
rubric.notes: A webcam / upload path (score your own capture live) would be a natural extension; noted as a follow-up.

rubric.evidence: Every number is the model's scalar output — the degrade slider is the honesty check: blur/darken visibly drops the score (0.386 -> 0.096 at blur-8), and the sharp original is always shown alongside for comparison. No canned scores, no identity claims (it doesn't recognise anyone).
rubric.notes: The gallery ranking is by the real scores; ties/near-ties are shown to 3 decimals so small differences are honest, not exaggerated.

rubric.evidence: The result is role=status aria-live=polite; the preview + gallery canvases carry role=img + aria-label; all controls are native + labelled with live value text; sliders are keyboard-operable.
rubric.notes: The quality bar colour (red->green) is decorative; the numeric score + 'degraded/no degradation' text carry the information for non-colour users.

rubric.evidence: The gallery is flex-wrap; face cards are fixed small thumbnails that wrap; the degrade row + preview wrap. Verified 0 horizontal overflow at desktop AND mobile 390px (validate-face-iqa.mjs), 0 console errors.
rubric.notes: Index verified desktop+mobile; subpage ladder + broader matrix are a recorded follow-up.

rubric.evidence: All inference runs in a dedicated module Worker; ~14 ms/face on single-thread WASM. Scoring the 6-face gallery + a live degrade slider stays responsive; the ~7 MB download is small.
rubric.notes: The degrade slider re-scores on each input; at 14 ms this is comfortably interactive without debouncing.

guidanceConsulted.query: run small ONNX scoring model off the main thread in a web worker; keep a live slider responsive
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move inference off the main thread; keep interactions responsive (INP); avoid blocking the UI on model work.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — scoring runs in a module Worker; at ~14 ms/face the live degrade slider re-scores per input without blocking; the gallery is scored asynchronously.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: page responsive during scoring; harness introspected the main thread while faces scored

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive thumbnail gallery + control row that wraps without horizontal overflow
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Prefer flex-wrap + intrinsic sizing over fixed widths; cap widths; let control rows wrap.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — .iqa-gallery + .iqa-degrade are flex-wrap; face cards are small fixed thumbnails; the preview + result wrap.

guidanceConsulted.query: announce a changing score via aria-live; label canvas content and sliders for assistive tech
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce the live result via role=status aria-live; label canvas content + every control; keep info off colour alone.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — result is role=status aria-live=polite; canvases have role=img + aria-label; sliders have labels + live value text; the score is numeric (not colour-only).
guidanceConsulted.evidence: result announces the quality + degradation state; the red->green bar is decorative

openQuestions: Is eDifFIQA's raw score usefully calibratable to an absolute 'good/bad' threshold, or is it only meaningful as a relative ranking within a session?
Would a real face-alignment step (the model expects aligned crops) meaningfully change scores vs the current centre-crop, and is a small aligner worth chaining in?
followUpGoals: Build the basics/practical/wild/multi-model ladder (e.g. practical: pick the best frame from a burst; wild: extreme pose/occlusion/backlight; multi-model: a face detector aligns crops before scoring).
Add a webcam path so users can watch their own live capture-quality score and get 'move closer / hold still' feedback.
Chain a face detector/aligner so arbitrary photos (not pre-cropped faces) can be scored correctly.
summary: Face image-quality assessment (eDifFIQA-T) — a genuinely new vision family (capture-quality scoring, distinct from detect/landmark/embed), proven to run + track degradation in the browser on ORT-web WASM (sharp 0.43 vs blur-8 0.11). Tiny (~7 MB), fast (~14 ms). Index demo complete + verified; subpage ladder is a recorded follow-up.
